test_cases.title: Palette displays all 6 component types
preconditions: Canvas Editor application is mounted
test_cases.given: The Canvas Editor is loaded and the left sidebar palette is rendered
test_cases.when: The palette component is inspected for its rendered items
test_cases.then: The palette contains exactly 6 items with labels: 'PLL', 'Divider', 'Mux', 'Clock Gate', 'IP Block', and 'Clock Domain', in any order

test_cases.title: Drag-and-drop from palette creates a node at drop position for each of the 6 component types
preconditions: Canvas Editor is open with an empty canvas
Backend API is available
test_cases.given: The palette is visible with all 6 component types and the canvas area is empty
test_cases.when: For each component type (PLL, Divider, Mux, Clock Gate, IP Block, Clock Domain), the user drags the item from the palette and drops it at a distinct canvas position
test_cases.then: For each drop, a new node of the corresponding type appears on the canvas at the drop position with correct default properties: PLL (output_freq, input_freq, name), Divider (ratio: 2, name), Mux (select_index: 0, name), Clock Gate (name), IP Block (power_mw, name), Clock Domain (domain_name, color, name); the canvas node count increases by 1 after each drop; and a POST request is sent to the backend node creation API for each dropped node

test_cases.title: Drop outside canvas area does not create a node (palette, property panel, toolbar)
preconditions: Canvas Editor is open with an empty canvas
test_cases.given: The palette is visible and the canvas has 0 nodes
test_cases.when: The user drags a 'Divider' item from the palette and drops it onto each of the following non-canvas areas in sequence: (1) the node palette sidebar, (2) the property panel area, (3) the toolbar area
test_cases.then: No new node is created on the canvas after any of the 3 drop attempts and the node count remains 0 after each attempt

test_cases.title: Clicking a node highlights it and updates the property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with at least one PLL node on the canvas
No node is currently selected
test_cases.given: The canvas contains a PLL node with id 'node-1' and the property panel shows no content
test_cases.when: The user clicks on the PLL node 'node-1'
test_cases.then: The node 'node-1' renders with a selection border (selected CSS class or outline), and the property panel displays the PLL node's properties including the 'name' field and 'output_freq' field

test_cases.title: Shift-click adds nodes to existing selection
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with at least two nodes: a PLL node 'node-1' and a Divider node 'node-2'
Node 'node-1' is already selected
test_cases.given: The canvas has PLL node 'node-1' selected and Divider node 'node-2' not selected
test_cases.when: The user holds Shift and clicks on Divider node 'node-2'
test_cases.then: Both 'node-1' and 'node-2' appear selected (both have selection borders) and neither node is deselected

test_cases.title: Clicking empty canvas area deselects all nodes and clears property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a PLL node 'node-1' that is currently selected
Property panel shows PLL node properties
test_cases.given: PLL node 'node-1' is selected and the property panel displays its properties
test_cases.when: The user clicks on an empty area of the canvas (no node or wire) without holding Shift
test_cases.then: Node 'node-1' loses its selection border, no node is selected, and the property panel displays no content (becomes empty)

test_cases.title: Delete key removes selected node and connected wires, then syncs to backend
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a PLL node 'node-1' and a Divider node 'node-2' connected by wire 'edge-1'
Node 'node-2' is selected
Backend API is available and interceptable
test_cases.given: Divider node 'node-2' is selected and wire 'edge-1' connects 'node-1' output to 'node-2' input
test_cases.when: The user presses the Delete key
test_cases.then: Node 'node-2' is removed from the canvas, wire 'edge-1' is also removed from the canvas, a DELETE request is sent to the backend API for node 'node-2', and a DELETE request is sent to the backend connection API for wire 'edge-1'

test_cases.title: Dragging a node updates its position and connected wires follow
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a PLL node at position (100, 100) and a Divider node at position (300, 100) connected by a wire
test_cases.given: PLL node 'node-1' is at position (100, 100) and connected to Divider 'node-2' via wire 'edge-1'
test_cases.when: The user drags 'node-1' from (100, 100) to (100, 300)
test_cases.then: Node 'node-1' position updates to (100, 300) and wire 'edge-1' path is recalculated so that it still connects 'node-1' output port to 'node-2' input port at the new position

test_cases.title: Dragging from output port to input port creates a wire and calls backend API
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a PLL node 'node-1' and a Divider node 'node-2' on the canvas with no existing connection
Backend API is available and interceptable
test_cases.given: PLL node 'node-1' and Divider node 'node-2' are on the canvas and not connected
test_cases.when: The user drags from the output port handle of 'node-1' to the input port handle of 'node-2'
test_cases.then: A wire appears connecting 'node-1' output to 'node-2' input, and a POST request is sent to the backend connection API containing source node 'node-1' and target node 'node-2'

test_cases.title: Dragging from output port shows preview wire and highlights compatible input ports
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a PLL node 'node-1' and a Divider node 'node-2' on the canvas
test_cases.given: PLL node 'node-1' and Divider node 'node-2' are on the canvas and the user begins dragging from the output port of 'node-1'
test_cases.when: The user is mid-drag (has not yet released the mouse) and the cursor is over an empty canvas area
test_cases.then: A preview wire is rendered following the cursor position, and the input port handle of 'node-2' (a compatible target) is visually highlighted (e.g., with a highlight color or enlarged handle)

test_cases.title: Connecting two output ports is rejected with an error toast
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a PLL node 'node-1' and a Divider node 'node-2' on the canvas
test_cases.given: PLL node 'node-1' and Divider node 'node-2' are on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user drags from the output port of 'node-1' and attempts to drop onto the output port of 'node-2'
test_cases.then: No wire is created, the preview wire disappears, and an error toast notification is displayed indicating the connection is invalid (output-to-output not allowed)

test_cases.title: Connecting two input ports is rejected with an error toast
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a Divider node 'node-1' and a Divider node 'node-2' on the canvas
test_cases.given: Two Divider nodes 'node-1' and 'node-2' are on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user drags from the input port of 'node-1' and attempts to drop onto the input port of 'node-2'
test_cases.then: No wire is created, the preview wire disappears, and an error toast notification is displayed indicating the connection is invalid (input-to-input not allowed)

test_cases.title: Connecting to an already-occupied input port is rejected with an error toast
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a PLL node 'node-1', a PLL node 'node-2', and a Divider node 'node-3'
Wire 'edge-1' already connects 'node-1' output to 'node-3' input
test_cases.given: Divider 'node-3' input port is already occupied by wire from 'node-1'
test_cases.when: The user drags from the output port of 'node-2' and attempts to drop onto the already-connected input port of 'node-3'
test_cases.then: No second wire is created, the preview wire disappears, and an error toast notification is displayed indicating the input port is already occupied

test_cases.title: Creating a cycle connection is rejected with an error toast
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a Divider node 'node-1' and a Divider node 'node-2'
Wire 'edge-1' connects 'node-1' output to 'node-2' input
test_cases.given: Divider 'node-1' output is already connected to Divider 'node-2' input via 'edge-1'
test_cases.when: The user drags from the output port of 'node-2' and attempts to drop onto the input port of 'node-1' (which would create a cycle)
test_cases.then: No wire is created, the preview wire disappears, and an error toast notification is displayed indicating a cycle would be created

test_cases.title: Selecting a wire and pressing Delete removes it and syncs to backend
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a PLL node 'node-1' and Divider node 'node-2' connected by wire 'edge-1'
Backend API is available and interceptable
test_cases.given: Wire 'edge-1' connects 'node-1' to 'node-2' and is not selected
test_cases.when: The user clicks on wire 'edge-1' to select it and then presses the Delete key
test_cases.then: Wire 'edge-1' is removed from the canvas and a DELETE request is sent to the backend disconnection API for 'edge-1'

test_cases.title: Selecting a PLL node shows name and output_freq fields in property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a PLL node 'node-pll' on the canvas
test_cases.given: A PLL node with output_freq=400 and input_freq=0 exists on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks on the PLL node
test_cases.then: The property panel displays: a 'name' text input field, an editable 'output_freq' numeric field showing 400, and a read-only 'input_freq' display field

test_cases.title: Selecting a Divider node shows name and ratio fields in property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a Divider node on the canvas
test_cases.given: A Divider node with ratio=2 exists on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks on the Divider node
test_cases.then: The property panel displays: a 'name' text input field and a 'ratio' field (dropdown or numeric) showing 2 with valid values {2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128}

test_cases.title: Selecting a Mux node shows name and select_index fields in property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a Mux node on the canvas
test_cases.given: A Mux node with select_index=0 exists on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks on the Mux node
test_cases.then: The property panel displays: a 'name' text input field and a 'select_index' numeric input field showing 0

test_cases.title: Selecting a Clock Gate node shows only name field in property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a Clock Gate node on the canvas
test_cases.given: A Clock Gate node exists on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks on the Clock Gate node
test_cases.then: The property panel displays: a 'name' text input field and no additional type-specific fields beyond 'name'

test_cases.title: Selecting an IP Block node shows name and power_mw fields in property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with an IP Block node on the canvas
test_cases.given: An IP Block node with power_mw=50 exists on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks on the IP Block node
test_cases.then: The property panel displays: a 'name' text input field and a 'power_mw' numeric input field showing 50

test_cases.title: Selecting a Clock Domain node shows name, domain_name, and color fields in property panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a Clock Domain node on the canvas
test_cases.given: A Clock Domain node with domain_name='fast_clk' and color='#FF0000' exists on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks on the Clock Domain node
test_cases.then: The property panel displays: a 'name' text input field, a 'domain_name' text input field showing 'fast_clk', and a 'color' color-picker or text input showing '#FF0000'

test_cases.title: Editing PLL output_freq updates node label, triggers API call, and recalculates downstream frequency
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a PLL node 'node-1' (output_freq=100) connected to a Divider node 'node-2' (ratio=2)
Divider 'node-2' currently displays frequency label '50 MHz' (100 / 2)
PLL node 'node-1' is selected
Property panel is visible showing output_freq=100
Backend API is available and interceptable
test_cases.given: PLL node 'node-1' is selected with output_freq=100, and a Divider node 'node-2' (ratio=2) is connected downstream showing '50 MHz'
test_cases.when: The user changes the output_freq input field to '400' and presses Enter (or the field loses focus)
test_cases.then: The PLL node label on the canvas updates to show '400 MHz', a PATCH request is sent to the backend API with the updated output_freq=400, and upon success the downstream Divider node 'node-2' frequency label updates to '200 MHz' (400 / 2 = 200)

test_cases.title: Divider node connected to PLL shows computed frequency label
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a PLL node 'node-pll' (output_freq=400) and a Divider node 'node-div' (ratio=2)
A wire connects 'node-pll' output to 'node-div' input
test_cases.given: PLL 'node-pll' has output_freq=400 MHz and Divider 'node-div' has ratio=2, connected by a wire
test_cases.when: The canvas renders the current graph state
test_cases.then: The Divider node 'node-div' displays a frequency label of '200 MHz' below the node (400 / 2 = 200)

test_cases.title: Scroll-up zoom does not exceed 400% maximum
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with current zoom level at 390%
test_cases.given: The canvas zoom level is at 390%
test_cases.when: The user scrolls the mouse wheel upward (zoom in) over the canvas
test_cases.then: The canvas zoom level increases but is clamped to a maximum of 400% and does not exceed 400%

test_cases.title: Scroll-down zoom does not go below 25% minimum
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with current zoom level at 30%
test_cases.given: The canvas zoom level is at 30%
test_cases.when: The user scrolls the mouse wheel downward (zoom out) over the canvas
test_cases.then: The canvas zoom level decreases but is clamped to a minimum of 25% and does not go below 25%

test_cases.title: Dragging on empty canvas area pans the viewport
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a known viewport transform (translateX=0, translateY=0)
test_cases.given: The canvas viewport is at transform (0, 0) and the cursor is over an empty area of the canvas
test_cases.when: The user presses the mouse button and drags 100px to the right and 50px downward on the empty canvas area
test_cases.then: The canvas viewport transform updates so that the view has moved (translateX increases by approximately 100 within ±10px tolerance, translateY increases by approximately 50 within ±10px tolerance), effectively panning the visible area

test_cases.title: Status bar counts update when nodes and connections are added or removed
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with an empty canvas (0 nodes, 0 connections, 0 CDC warnings)
Status bar is visible at the bottom
test_cases.given: The status bar shows 'Nodes: 0, Connections: 0, CDC Warnings: 0'
test_cases.when: Two nodes are added to the canvas and one wire is drawn connecting them
test_cases.then: The status bar updates in real-time to show 'Nodes: 2, Connections: 1, CDC Warnings: 0'

test_cases.title: Save button on existing project sends canvas state to backend and shows success notification
preconditions: Canvas Editor has an existing project loaded (project id='proj-1') with 2 nodes and 1 connection
Backend save API is available and interceptable
test_cases.given: Project 'proj-1' is loaded with 2 nodes and 1 connection on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Save button in the toolbar
test_cases.then: A PUT request is sent to the backend save API with the full canvas state (nodes with positions and properties, all connections), and a success toast notification appears

test_cases.title: Load button fetches project list, shows dialog, and loads selected project
preconditions: Canvas Editor is open with an empty canvas
Backend returns a project list: [{id: 'proj-1', name: 'My Design'}]
test_cases.given: The canvas is empty and the backend has one saved project 'My Design'
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Load button in the toolbar, then selects 'My Design' from the project selection dialog
test_cases.then: The project selection dialog appears with 'My Design' listed, after selection the dialog closes, the canvas is populated with the project's nodes and connections, replacing any previous canvas state

test_cases.title: Export button triggers JSON file download of the current design
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a project loaded with 1 PLL node
Backend export API is available and returns design JSON
test_cases.given: A project with 1 PLL node is loaded on the canvas
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Export button in the toolbar
test_cases.then: A GET request is sent to the backend export API and the browser initiates a file download of a JSON file containing the design data

test_cases.title: Import button uploads selected JSON file and loads the design onto the canvas
preconditions: Canvas Editor is open with an empty canvas
A valid design JSON file 'design.json' is available on the local filesystem
Backend import API is available
test_cases.given: The canvas is empty and a valid design JSON file exists locally
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Import button in the toolbar and selects 'design.json' in the file picker
test_cases.then: The file is uploaded to the backend import API, upon success response the canvas is populated with the imported design's nodes and connections

test_cases.title: Generate button shows code preview dialog then downloads ZIP on Download click
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a design with at least 1 PLL node and 1 IP Block connected
Backend code generation preview API returns Verilog and SDC strings
test_cases.given: A valid design is loaded on the canvas with a PLL connected to an IP Block
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Generate button in the toolbar, reviews the code preview dialog showing Verilog and SDC code with syntax highlighting, then clicks the Download button within the dialog
test_cases.then: The code preview dialog appears with Verilog and SDC content, syntax highlighting is applied, and clicking Download triggers a ZIP file download containing the generated RTL and SDC files

test_cases.title: CDC Check button shows crossing highlights on canvas and summary panel
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a design with two Clock Domain nodes at different frequencies connected via a direct wire (creating a CDC crossing)
Backend CDC analysis API returns crossing data
test_cases.given: The canvas contains nodes from two different clock domains with a direct connection crossing domain boundaries
test_cases.when: The user clicks the CDC Check button in the toolbar
test_cases.then: The backend CDC analysis API is called, the canvas highlights the crossing wire(s) (e.g., in red or orange), and a summary panel appears listing source domain, target domain, and affected nodes for each crossing

test_cases.title: Gating Analysis button shows summary dialog with gated/ungated IP counts and power reduction
preconditions: Canvas Editor has a design with 2 IP Blocks: 1 behind a Clock Gate (gated) and 1 directly connected (ungated)
Backend gating analysis API returns: gated_count=1, ungated_count=1, total_count=2, power_reduction_pct=50
test_cases.given: The canvas design has 1 gated IP Block and 1 ungated IP Block
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Gating Analysis button in the toolbar
test_cases.then: The backend gating analysis API is called and a summary dialog appears showing: gated_count=1, ungated_count=1, total_count=2, and power_reduction_pct=50%

test_cases.title: Backend API network error shows error toast and reverts optimistic UI update
preconditions: Canvas Editor is open with an empty canvas
Network is intercepted so all backend API requests return a network error
test_cases.given: The canvas is empty and the network is blocked so any API call will fail
test_cases.when: The user drags a PLL node from the palette and drops it onto the canvas (triggering an optimistic UI add + backend API call)
test_cases.then: The node briefly appears on the canvas (optimistic update), the API call fails, a toast notification appears with the error message, and the node is removed from the canvas reverting to the pre-action state (0 nodes)

test_cases.title: Save on new canvas prompts for project name, creates project via POST, then saves
preconditions: Canvas Editor is open with a new empty canvas (no project loaded, no project id)
Canvas has 1 PLL node added
Backend POST /api/projects and save APIs are available and interceptable
test_cases.given: The canvas is in new (unsaved) state with 1 PLL node and no project id
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Save button in the toolbar, enters 'My New Design' in the project name dialog, and confirms
test_cases.then: A project name input dialog appears, after confirmation a POST request is sent to '/api/projects' with name='My New Design', upon success the canvas state is saved to the newly created project, and a success toast notification appears

test_cases.title: Mux sel port and Clock Gate en port render as connectable port handles on canvas
preconditions: Canvas Editor is rendered with a Mux node 'node-mux' and a Clock Gate node 'node-gate' on the canvas
test_cases.given: A Mux node 'node-mux' and a Clock Gate node 'node-gate' are displayed on the canvas
test_cases.when: The canvas renders both nodes with their port handles visible
test_cases.then: The Mux node displays a 'sel' port handle that is visually distinct and connectable (accepts wire drag), and the Clock Gate node displays an 'en' port handle that is visually distinct and connectable (accepts wire drag), in addition to their standard input and output port handles

test_cases.title: New 버튼 클릭 시 캔버스가 초기화된다
test_cases.given: 사용자가 노드와 연결선이 있는 프로젝트를 캔버스에 로드한 상태
test_cases.when: 사용자가 툴바의 New 버튼을 클릭한다
test_cases.then: 캔버스의 모든 노드와 연결선이 제거되고, 프로젝트 상태가 '저장되지 않음'으로 초기화되며, 빈 캔버스가 표시된다

test_cases.title: Load 다이얼로그에서 프로젝트를 삭제할 수 있다
test_cases.given: 저장된 프로젝트가 하나 이상 존재하고, 사용자가 Load 다이얼로그를 열었다
test_cases.when: 사용자가 프로젝트 항목의 Delete 버튼을 클릭하고 확인 프롬프트에서 확인한다
test_cases.then: 해당 프로젝트가 백엔드 API를 통해 삭제되고 프로젝트 목록에서 제거된다

test_cases.title: CDC Check 실행 후 상태 바의 CDC 경고 수가 갱신된다
test_cases.given: 두 개의 ClockDomain 노드가 서로 다른 도메인 이름으로 설정되어 있고, 그 사이에 클럭 신호가 연결되어 CDC 교차가 발생하는 설계가 캔버스에 있다
test_cases.when: 사용자가 툴바의 CDC Check 버튼을 클릭한다
test_cases.then: 하단 상태 바의 CDC Warnings 카운트가 0에서 1 이상으로 증가한다
